pages.full_text:
80 having parked the van in a place reserved for buses.
	That night we camped in a very good site in St. Illiers [?en] Ville Domaine de Inchelin (1) 34-76-10-11 * r***
There were hedges between the plots, an oak wood, cages of birds, old barns & a swimming pool. John (3yrs & 11 mo) slept in a tent for the first time with Thomas. The boys went for a swim.
July 13th
	We finally made it to Chartres & shopped in the market where John's & Charles' sunglasses kept breaking.
	In the cathedral the redoubtable Mr. Miller is still lecturing. He shsed Charles when I pulled him down, screaming, from the base of a pier. The floor of the cathedral is paved in lovely old slabs.
	We had a good meal & left on the Paris road to find a supermarket. Then we went back through Chartres, to
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence continuing from previous spread. 'St. Illiers en Ville' — the 'en' is written as a small superscript correction above 'on'; could also read 'a' or 'en'. Phone number '(1) 34-76-10-11' follows the campsite name. Year not stated on page; volume covers 1989-2003 and children's ages (John 3yrs 11mo) may help pin year but cannot confirm from page alone — iso_guess year left tentative. 'shsed' appears to be 'shushed' abbreviated/misspelled as written.

pages.full_text:
__Chartres__
g off towards Tours.
	On the way we've seen a windmill, & stopped to look at it. The sails make a whirring noise. Inside it smelled of dust & flour. Lots of creaking & wood machinery noises. The machines are partly controlled by wood batons & string. The whole building trembles, shudders rhythmically, like being on a ship, but a much faster rhythm.
pages.route_sketch_description: Pencil sketch of Chartres Cathedral showing the two towers and rose window, with a smaller attached structure to the right. No arrows or route lines — purely an architectural illustration labelled 'Chartres' (underlined) below the drawing.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence continuing from left page — 'g off towards Tours' is the tail end of 'going off towards Tours' or similar. The sketch at the top of the page is a hand-drawn pencil illustration of Chartres Cathedral rather than a route map, but flagged as route_sketch true because it is a hand-drawn geographic/architectural image with a place label. The word 'smelled' could also be read as 'smells' — written form is ambiguous.
